reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (33 + 27) is trivial, so I’m sure my part is correct. In all previous games, every initial arithmetic capsule has been correct and we’ve never seen conflicting answers. With only one capsule per question, the likelihood the full password is right is extremely high. Verifying would just burn coins, and passing risks a random death if nobody volunteers, as happened in Game 1. Volunteering now is the safest way to secure a quick escape and keep all 4 of my coins.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle (26 + 58) is straightforward, so I’m certain my own answer in Q2-B is correct. All previous games have been simple arithmetic with no incorrect answers so far, and there is exactly one capsule per question with no conflicts, so the chance any of them is wrong is extremely low. Verifying would just waste a coin while still leaving almost the same residual risk, and if everyone hesitates we face the possibility of no volunteer and a 1-in-4 random death. Volunteering now with the only available capsules maximizes my survival odds and lets me keep all 4 coins if, as is overwhelmingly likely, the password is correct.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own puzzle (17 + 19) is trivial, so I’m fully confident my capsule Q3-C is correct. Across all previous games, every player’s arithmetic answer has been correct whenever revealed, so the chance that A, B, or D made a mistake on similarly simple puzzles is extremely small. Verifying would cost a coin and I still couldn’t verify everything in this round, while also leaving open the risk that no one volunteers and we face a 25% random-death lottery as in Game 1. Volunteering immediately with the only available capsules gives a very high chance of instant escape while keeping all 4 of my coins.
